justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Distributed tracing gap explicitly identified as cross-service diagnosis blocker in component_interactions and escalations. (2) 30-second log delay called out as incident response concern — 'debugging the state of the world 30 seconds ago.' (3) Stale runbook flagged with strong language — 'arguably worse than no runbook.' (4) No recovery tooling for stuck orders identified as highest-impact gap, with outbox/event-log recommendation. (5) Multiple specific recommendations: tracing with W3C trace context, runbook update, outbox table, stuck-order detector, circuit breakers, admin tooling. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on the silent-stuckness failure mode — identifying that alerting only on failed transitions misses the most dangerous case (transitions never attempted) goes beyond the rubric requirements

Strong operational framing throughout — '15-30 minutes of manual log spelunking before forming a hypothesis' and 'ad-hoc SQL against production at 3am' ground the assessment in real incident response reality
Correctly notes alert routing via PagerDuty is adequate while identifying that alert context is insufficient — nuanced rather than binary assessment
Identifies additional gaps beyond rubric requirements: circuit breakers, retry/timeout config, rollback safety for state machine services, partial failure scenarios
Log delay assessment is well-calibrated — acknowledges it depends on incident velocity rather than treating it as universally critical
Confidence section is honest about assumptions and low-confidence areas, particularly that retry/timeout config may exist but wasn't described
